content:
Method 1: (In the email list) select the email by long-pressing it or by tapping the icon before the email > tap the vertical three dots on the upper right corner > "Move".

Method 2: (Inside the email) tap the vertical three dots on the upper right corner > "Move".

Method 3: 3.1. You can configure swipe actions to "Move": "Settings" > "General settings" > "Interaction" > "Swipe actions" > configure action for "Right swipe" or "Left swipe" to "Move". 3.2. Then you can right-swipe or left-swipe the email to move it.

- - - - - -

If you do not see "Move" after tapping the vertical three dots, the following steps might be helpful: "Settings" > "General settings" > "Display" > "Visible message actions" > tick "Move" > "OK".
creator: Yu5tiqX9og

content:
Do you use POP recent mode or POP normal mode? If you want to use Gmail POP on multiple devices, Google recommends using POP recent mode instead of POP normal mode.

The following links might be helpful: 1. How does Gmail POP work? https://support.google.com/a/answer/6089246 2. Read Gmail messages on other email clients using POP https://support.google.com/mail/answer/7104828

Quotation:

Gmail users can use either POP normal mode or recent mode to sync their mail. If you're syncing your mail to one mail client, you should use normal mode. If you're syncing mail to a few different mail clients, then you should use recent mode.

content:
Hi Sam,

It sounds like there may be an issue with the POP account syncing properly after import. Here are a few things you can check:

Verify POP Settings – Make sure your Gmail account is set to allow POP access in Gmail settings (under Forwarding and POP/IMAP). Check Account Configuration – Go to Account Settings in TB and ensure the POP server settings are correct: Incoming server: pop.gmail.com Port: 995 Connection security: SSL/TLS Authentication: OAuth2 (preferred) or Normal Password Manually Download Messages – Try selecting Get Messages or restarting TB to trigger a download. Check for Duplicate Profiles – If you've imported multiple times, there might be duplicate configurations causing conflicts. Try Adding the Account Manually – Instead of importing, try setting up the Gmail POP account manually in TB. If the issue persists, you might also check TB logs (Tools > Activity Manager) for any errors. Let me know how it goes!

Best, Colin Munro
creator: icolinmunro

content:
(a) It seems that there must exist a folder for archiving emails so that archiving emails can work.

The following steps will set the folder for archiving emails: "Settings" > "General settings" > "Accounts" section > tap the account you want to configure > "Folders" > "Archive folder" > select a folder (usually "Archive" folder). Do not select "None" or "Automatic (None)".

(b) Methods to archive an email:

Method 1: (In the email list) select the email by long-pressing it or by tapping the icon before the email > tap the "Archive" icon above the email list.

Method 2: (Inside the email) tap the "Archive" icon above the email.

If you do not see "Archive" icon above the email, the following steps might be helpful: "Settings" > "General settings" > "Display" > "Visible message actions" > tick "Archive" > "OK".

Method 3: (Inside the email) tap the vertical three dots on the upper right corner > "Refile..." > "Archive".

Method 4: 4.1. You can configure swipe actions to "Archive": "Settings" > "General settings" > "Interaction" > "Swipe actions" > configure action for "Right swipe" or "Left swipe" to "Archive". 4.2. Then you can right-swipe or left-swipe the email to archive it.
creator: Yu5tiqX9og
